Detailed_Business_Rule: Executes the daily transaction posting program. The job allocates one master transaction file (TRANFILE), one input daily transaction sequential file (DALYTRAN), one card cross-reference file (XREFFILE), one account master file (ACCTFILE), one transaction-category-balance file (TCATBALF), and one new generation-data-group reject output (DALYREJS+1). The new daily rejects dataset is allocated with NEW,CATLG,DELETE disposition, fixed-length 430-byte records, and one cylinder of primary space.
Review_Comments: Hardcoded DSN: AWS.M2.CARDDEMO.LOADLIB, AWS.M2.CARDDEMO.TRANSACT.VSAM.KSDS, AWS.M2.CARDDEMO.DALYTRAN.PS, AWS.M2.CARDDEMO.CARDXREF.VSAM.KSDS, AWS.M2.CARDDEMO.ACCTDATA.VSAM.KSDS, AWS.M2.CARDDEMO.TCATBALF.VSAM.KSDS; GDG reference: DALYREJS(+1); replace VSAM KSDS with PostgreSQL/DynamoDB and PS files with S3 objects

Input_File_Layout: DALYTRAN-ID(PIC X(16))|DALYTRAN-TYPE-CD(PIC X(02))|DALYTRAN-CAT-CD(PIC 9(04))|DALYTRAN-SOURCE(PIC X(10))|DALYTRAN-DESC(PIC X(100))|DALYTRAN-AMT(PIC S9(09)V99)|DALYTRAN-MERCHANT-ID(PIC 9(09))|DALYTRAN-MERCHANT-NAME(PIC X(50))|DALYTRAN-MERCHANT-CITY(PIC X(50))|DALYTRAN-MERCHANT-ZIP(PIC X(10))|DALYTRAN-CARD-NUM(PIC X(16))|DALYTRAN-ORIG-TS(PIC X(26))|DALYTRAN-PROC-TS(PIC X(26))|FILLER(PIC X(20))
Detailed_Business_Rule: Opens the daily transaction sequential input file. If the open returns COBOL file-status code 00 the program proceeds; any other status displays an error message and abends the program with completion code 999. This is a hard failure path: a missing or corrupt daily transaction file terminates the entire run.
SQL_Decision_Control_Statements:
OPEN INPUT DALYTRAN-FILE
IF DALYTRAN-STATUS = '00'
    MOVE 0 TO APPL-RESULT
ELSE
    MOVE 12 TO APPL-RESULT
END-IF
IF NOT APPL-AOK
    DISPLAY 'ERROR OPENING DALYTRAN'
    PERFORM 9999-ABEND-PROGRAM

Detailed_Business_Rule: Opens the master transaction VSAM KSDS for I-O so the program can both write new posted transactions and (in error paths) rewrite existing entries. Indexed file with RECORD KEY FD-TRANS-ID and random access mode. A non-zero file status terminates the program with completion code 999.
SQL_Decision_Control_Statements:
OPEN I-O TRANSACT-FILE
IF TRANFILE-STATUS = '00'
    MOVE 0 TO APPL-RESULT
ELSE
    MOVE 12 TO APPL-RESULT
    PERFORM 9999-ABEND-PROGRAM

Detailed_Business_Rule: Opens the card cross-reference VSAM KSDS for INPUT only. The cross-reference is keyed by card number and is used to translate the daily transaction's card number to its owning account ID. A non-zero file status terminates the program with completion code 999.
SQL_Decision_Control_Statements:
OPEN INPUT XREF-FILE
IF XREFFILE-STATUS = '00'
    MOVE 0 TO APPL-RESULT
ELSE
    MOVE 12 TO APPL-RESULT
    PERFORM 9999-ABEND-PROGRAM

Detailed_Business_Rule: Opens the daily rejects sequential output file. Every rejected daily transaction (with reject reason code and description) is appended here for downstream operations review. A non-zero file status terminates the program with completion code 999.
SQL_Decision_Control_Statements:
OPEN OUTPUT DALYREJS-FILE
IF DALYREJS-STATUS = '00'
    MOVE 0 TO APPL-RESULT
ELSE
    MOVE 12 TO APPL-RESULT
    PERFORM 9999-ABEND-PROGRAM

Detailed_Business_Rule: Opens the account master VSAM KSDS for I-O so the program can read accounts to validate balance/expiration and rewrite the same record after applying the posted transaction's signed amount to the account's running balance and cycle credit/debit. A non-zero file status terminates the program with completion code 999.

OPEN I-O ACCOUNT-FILE
IF ACCTFILE-STATUS = '00'
    MOVE 0 TO APPL-RESULT
ELSE

A non-zero file status terminates the program with completion code 999.

OPEN I-O TCATBAL-FILE
IF TCATBALF-STATUS = '00'
    MOVE 0 TO APPL-RESULT
ELSE

Input_File_Layout: DALYTRAN-ID(PIC X(16))|DALYTRAN-TYPE-CD(PIC X(02))|DALYTRAN-CAT-CD(PIC 9(04))|DALYTRAN-SOURCE(PIC X(10))|DALYTRAN-DESC(PIC X(100))|DALYTRAN-AMT(PIC S9(09)V99)|DALYTRAN-MERCHANT-ID(PIC 9(09))|DALYTRAN-MERCHANT-NAME(PIC X(50))|DALYTRAN-MERCHANT-CITY(PIC X(50))|DALYTRAN-MERCHANT-ZIP(PIC X(10))|DALYTRAN-CARD-NUM(PIC X(16))|DALYTRAN-ORIG-TS(PIC X(26))|DALYTRAN-PROC-TS(PIC X(26))|FILLER(PIC X(20))
Detailed_Business_Rule: Top of the processing loop. While the daily transaction file is not at end-of-file the program reads the next daily transaction record. The transaction count is incremented by one for every successfully read record so a final processed/rejected summary can be displayed at end-of-job.
SQL_Decision_Control_Statements:
PERFORM UNTIL END-OF-FILE = 'Y'
    IF END-OF-FILE = 'N'
        PERFORM 1000-DALYTRAN-GET-NEXT
        IF END-OF-FILE = 'N'
            ADD 1 TO WS-TRANSACTION-COUNT

Input_File_Layout: DALYTRAN-ID(PIC X(16))|DALYTRAN-TYPE-CD(PIC X(02))|DALYTRAN-CAT-CD(PIC 9(04))|DALYTRAN-SOURCE(PIC X(10))|DALYTRAN-DESC(PIC X(100))|DALYTRAN-AMT(PIC S9(09)V99)|DALYTRAN-MERCHANT-ID(PIC 9(09))|DALYTRAN-MERCHANT-NAME(PIC X(50))|DALYTRAN-MERCHANT-CITY(PIC X(50))|DALYTRAN-MERCHANT-ZIP(PIC X(10))|DALYTRAN-CARD-NUM(PIC X(16))|DALYTRAN-ORIG-TS(PIC X(26))|DALYTRAN-PROC-TS(PIC X(26))|FILLER(PIC X(20))
Detailed_Business_Rule: Reads the next sequential record from the daily transaction file. File status 00 is treated as a successful read; status 10 marks end-of-file and sets the END-OF-FILE flag to Y so the main loop exits cleanly; any other status displays an I/O error, prints the file status, and abends the program with completion code 999.
SQL_Decision_Control_Statements:
READ DALYTRAN-FILE INTO DALYTRAN-RECORD
IF DALYTRAN-STATUS = '00'
    MOVE 0 TO APPL-RESULT
ELSE IF DALYTRAN-STATUS = '10'
    MOVE 16 TO APPL-RESULT
ELSE
    MOVE 12 TO APPL-RESULT
IF APPL-EOF
    MOVE 'Y' TO END-OF-FILE
ELSE
    DISPLAY 'ERROR READING DAILY TRANSACTION FILE'
    PERFORM 9999-ABEND-PROGRAM

Input_File_Layout: DALYTRAN-ID(PIC X(16))|DALYTRAN-TYPE-CD(PIC X(02))|DALYTRAN-CAT-CD(PIC 9(04))|DALYTRAN-SOURCE(PIC X(10))|DALYTRAN-DESC(PIC X(100))|DALYTRAN-AMT(PIC S9(09)V99)|DALYTRAN-MERCHANT-ID(PIC 9(09))|DALYTRAN-MERCHANT-NAME(PIC X(50))|DALYTRAN-MERCHANT-CITY(PIC X(50))|DALYTRAN-MERCHANT-ZIP(PIC X(10))|DALYTRAN-CARD-NUM(PIC X(16))|DALYTRAN-ORIG-TS(PIC X(26))|DALYTRAN-PROC-TS(PIC X(26))|FILLER(PIC X(20))
Detailed_Business_Rule: Initializes the validation outcome to zero (zero meaning no failure) before dispatching to the validation paragraph. After validation, the program either posts the transaction if no failure was recorded or writes a reject record and increments the reject counter.
SQL_Decision_Control_Statements:
MOVE 0 TO WS-VALIDATION-FAIL-REASON
MOVE SPACES TO WS-VALIDATION-FAIL-REASON-DESC
PERFORM 1500-VALIDATE-TRAN
IF WS-VALIDATION-FAIL-REASON = 0
    PERFORM 2000-POST-TRANSACTION
ELSE
    ADD 1 TO WS-REJECT-COUNT
    PERFORM 2500-WRITE-REJECT-REC
END-IF

Input_File_Layout: DALYTRAN-ID(PIC X(16))|DALYTRAN-TYPE-CD(PIC X(02))|DALYTRAN-CAT-CD(PIC 9(04))|DALYTRAN-SOURCE(PIC X(10))|DALYTRAN-DESC(PIC X(100))|DALYTRAN-AMT(PIC S9(09)V99)|DALYTRAN-MERCHANT-ID(PIC 9(09))|DALYTRAN-MERCHANT-NAME(PIC X(50))|DALYTRAN-MERCHANT-CITY(PIC X(50))|DALYTRAN-MERCHANT-ZIP(PIC X(10))|DALYTRAN-CARD-NUM(PIC X(16))|DALYTRAN-ORIG-TS(PIC X(26))|DALYTRAN-PROC-TS(PIC X(26))|FILLER(PIC X(20))
Detailed_Business_Rule: Validation orchestrator. Calls the cross-reference lookup first; only if that succeeds does it call the account lookup and credit-limit/expiration validation. Stops after the first failure so that downstream lookups are skipped when the card or account is unknown.
SQL_Decision_Control_Statements:
PERFORM 1500-A-LOOKUP-XREF
IF WS-VALIDATION-FAIL-REASON = 0
    PERFORM 1500-B-LOOKUP-ACCT
ELSE
    CONTINUE
END-IF

Input_File_Layout: DALYTRAN-ID(PIC X(16))|DALYTRAN-TYPE-CD(PIC X(02))|DALYTRAN-CAT-CD(PIC 9(04))|DALYTRAN-SOURCE(PIC X(10))|DALYTRAN-DESC(PIC X(100))|DALYTRAN-AMT(PIC S9(09)V99)|DALYTRAN-MERCHANT-ID(PIC 9(09))|DALYTRAN-MERCHANT-NAME(PIC X(50))|DALYTRAN-MERCHANT-CITY(PIC X(50))|DALYTRAN-MERCHANT-ZIP(PIC X(10))|DALYTRAN-CARD-NUM(PIC X(16))|DALYTRAN-ORIG-TS(PIC X(26))|DALYTRAN-PROC-TS(PIC X(26))|FILLER(PIC X(20)) (input)|XREF-CARD-NUM(PIC X(16))|XREF-CUST-ID(PIC 9(09))|XREF-ACCT-ID(PIC 9(11))|FILLER(PIC X(14)) (lookup)
Detailed_Business_Rule: Card cross-reference lookup. The daily transaction's 16-digit card number is used as the key to read the card cross-reference file. If the card number is not found (INVALID KEY path) the validation reason code is set to 100 with description 'INVALID CARD NUMBER FOUND' and the transaction is rejected. On a successful lookup the cross-reference record is in memory so the owning account ID is available for the next validation step.
SQL_Decision_Control_Statements:
MOVE DALYTRAN-CARD-NUM TO FD-XREF-CARD-NUM
READ XREF-FILE INTO CARD-XREF-RECORD
    INVALID KEY
        MOVE 100 TO WS-VALIDATION-FAIL-REASON
        MOVE 'INVALID CARD NUMBER FOUND' TO WS-VALIDATION-FAIL-REASON-DESC
    NOT INVALID KEY
        CONTINUE
END-READ
Review_Comments: Reject reason code 100 is hardcoded. Reject description 'INVALID CARD NUMBER FOUND' is hardcoded English text.

Input_File_Layout: XREF-CARD-NUM(PIC X(16))|XREF-CUST-ID(PIC 9(09))|XREF-ACCT-ID(PIC 9(11))|FILLER(PIC X(14)) (carry)|ACCT-ID(PIC 9(11))|ACCT-ACTIVE-STATUS(PIC X(01))|ACCT-CURR-BAL(PIC S9(10)V99)|ACCT-CREDIT-LIMIT(PIC S9(10)V99)|ACCT-CASH-CREDIT-LIMIT(PIC S9(10)V99)|ACCT-OPEN-DATE(PIC X(10))|ACCT-EXPIRAION-DATE(PIC X(10))|ACCT-REISSUE-DATE(PIC X(10))|ACCT-CURR-CYC-CREDIT(PIC S9(10)V99)|ACCT-CURR-CYC-DEBIT(PIC S9(10)V99)|ACCT-ADDR-ZIP(PIC X(10))|ACCT-GROUP-ID(PIC X(10))|FILLER(PIC X(178)) (lookup)
Detailed_Business_Rule: Account master lookup. The cross-reference's account ID is used as the key to read the account record. If the account is not found (INVALID KEY path) the validation reason code is set to 101 with description 'ACCOUNT RECORD NOT FOUND' and the transaction is rejected. On a successful lookup the account record (current balance, credit limit, cycle credit, cycle debit, expiration date) is in memory for credit-limit and expiration checks.
SQL_Decision_Control_Statements:
MOVE XREF-ACCT-ID TO FD-ACCT-ID
READ ACCOUNT-FILE INTO ACCOUNT-RECORD
    INVALID KEY
        MOVE 101 TO WS-VALIDATION-FAIL-REASON
        MOVE 'ACCOUNT RECORD NOT FOUND' TO WS-VALIDATION-FAIL-REASON-DESC
Review_Comments: Reject reason code 101 is hardcoded. Reject description 'ACCOUNT RECORD NOT FOUND' is hardcoded English text.

Input_File_Layout: ACCT-ID(PIC 9(11))|ACCT-ACTIVE-STATUS(PIC X(01))|ACCT-CURR-BAL(PIC S9(10)V99)|ACCT-CREDIT-LIMIT(PIC S9(10)V99)|ACCT-CASH-CREDIT-LIMIT(PIC S9(10)V99)|ACCT-OPEN-DATE(PIC X(10))|ACCT-EXPIRAION-DATE(PIC X(10))|ACCT-REISSUE-DATE(PIC X(10))|ACCT-CURR-CYC-CREDIT(PIC S9(10)V99)|ACCT-CURR-CYC-DEBIT(PIC S9(10)V99)|ACCT-ADDR-ZIP(PIC X(10))|ACCT-GROUP-ID(PIC X(10))|FILLER(PIC X(178))|DALYTRAN-ID(PIC X(16))|DALYTRAN-TYPE-CD(PIC X(02))|DALYTRAN-CAT-CD(PIC 9(04))|DALYTRAN-SOURCE(PIC X(10))|DALYTRAN-DESC(PIC X(100))|DALYTRAN-AMT(PIC S9(09)V99)|DALYTRAN-MERCHANT-ID(PIC 9(09))|DALYTRAN-MERCHANT-NAME(PIC X(50))|DALYTRAN-MERCHANT-CITY(PIC X(50))|DALYTRAN-MERCHANT-ZIP(PIC X(10))|DALYTRAN-CARD-NUM(PIC X(16))|DALYTRAN-ORIG-TS(PIC X(26))|DALYTRAN-PROC-TS(PIC X(26))|FILLER(PIC X(20))
Detailed_Business_Rule: Computes the running cycle balance after applying the new daily transaction amount. The formula is: running cycle balance equals current cycle credit minus current cycle debit plus the new daily transaction amount. The result is held in working storage for the credit-limit comparison that follows.
SQL_Decision_Control_Statements: COMPUTE WS-TEMP-BAL = ACCT-CURR-CYC-CREDIT\n                    - ACCT-CURR-CYC-DEBIT\n                    + DALYTRAN-AMT
Review_Comments: Packed-decimal COMP-3 arithmetic across PIC S9(10)V99 fields; map to BigDecimal with MathContext.DECIMAL64 in Java migration.

Input_File_Layout: ACCT-ID(PIC 9(11))|ACCT-ACTIVE-STATUS(PIC X(01))|ACCT-CURR-BAL(PIC S9(10)V99)|ACCT-CREDIT-LIMIT(PIC S9(10)V99)|ACCT-CASH-CREDIT-LIMIT(PIC S9(10)V99)|ACCT-OPEN-DATE(PIC X(10))|ACCT-EXPIRAION-DATE(PIC X(10))|ACCT-REISSUE-DATE(PIC X(10))|ACCT-CURR-CYC-CREDIT(PIC S9(10)V99)|ACCT-CURR-CYC-DEBIT(PIC S9(10)V99)|ACCT-ADDR-ZIP(PIC X(10))|ACCT-GROUP-ID(PIC X(10))|FILLER(PIC X(178))
Detailed_Business_Rule: Credit-limit validation. The transaction is rejected when the account's posted credit limit is less than the running cycle balance. In other words, if applying this new transaction would push the cycle's net activity above the account's credit limit, the transaction is rejected with reason code 102 and description 'OVERLIMIT TRANSACTION'.
SQL_Decision_Control_Statements:
IF ACCT-CREDIT-LIMIT >= WS-TEMP-BAL
    CONTINUE
ELSE
    MOVE 102 TO WS-VALIDATION-FAIL-REASON
    MOVE 'OVERLIMIT TRANSACTION' TO WS-VALIDATION-FAIL-REASON-DESC
END-IF
Review_Comments: Reject reason code 102 is hardcoded. Reject description 'OVERLIMIT TRANSACTION' is hardcoded English text.

Input_File_Layout: ACCT-ID(PIC 9(11))|ACCT-ACTIVE-STATUS(PIC X(01))|ACCT-CURR-BAL(PIC S9(10)V99)|ACCT-CREDIT-LIMIT(PIC S9(10)V99)|ACCT-CASH-CREDIT-LIMIT(PIC S9(10)V99)|ACCT-OPEN-DATE(PIC X(10))|ACCT-EXPIRAION-DATE(PIC X(10))|ACCT-REISSUE-DATE(PIC X(10))|ACCT-CURR-CYC-CREDIT(PIC S9(10)V99)|ACCT-CURR-CYC-DEBIT(PIC S9(10)V99)|ACCT-ADDR-ZIP(PIC X(10))|ACCT-GROUP-ID(PIC X(10))|FILLER(PIC X(178))|DALYTRAN-ID(PIC X(16))|DALYTRAN-TYPE-CD(PIC X(02))|DALYTRAN-CAT-CD(PIC 9(04))|DALYTRAN-SOURCE(PIC X(10))|DALYTRAN-DESC(PIC X(100))|DALYTRAN-AMT(PIC S9(09)V99)|DALYTRAN-MERCHANT-ID(PIC 9(09))|DALYTRAN-MERCHANT-NAME(PIC X(50))|DALYTRAN-MERCHANT-CITY(PIC X(50))|DALYTRAN-MERCHANT-ZIP(PIC X(10))|DALYTRAN-CARD-NUM(PIC X(16))|DALYTRAN-ORIG-TS(PIC X(26))|DALYTRAN-PROC-TS(PIC X(26))|FILLER(PIC X(20))
Detailed_Business_Rule: Expiration date validation. The first 10 characters of the daily transaction's origination timestamp are compared character-wise to the account's expiration date in YYYY-MM-DD form. If the transaction's origination date falls after the account's expiration date the transaction is rejected with reason code 103 and description 'TRANSACTION RECEIVED AFTER ACCT EXPIRATION'.
SQL_Decision_Control_Statements:
IF ACCT-EXPIRAION-DATE >= DALYTRAN-ORIG-TS (1:10)
    CONTINUE
ELSE
    MOVE 103 TO WS-VALIDATION-FAIL-REASON
    MOVE 'TRANSACTION RECEIVED AFTER ACCT EXPIRATION' TO WS-VALIDATION-FAIL-REASON-DESC
END-IF
Review_Comments: Reject reason code 103 is hardcoded. Comparison is character-wise across YYYY-MM-DD prefix; consider full timestamp comparison or LocalDate parse-and-compare in Java migration.

Detailed_Business_Rule: Maps every business field from the daily-transaction record onto the master transaction record (transaction id, type, category, source, description, amount, merchant id/name/city/zip, card number, origination timestamp). The processing timestamp is set to the current DB2-format timestamp.
SQL_Decision_Control_Statements:
MOVE DALYTRAN-ID TO TRAN-ID
MOVE DALYTRAN-TYPE-CD TO TRAN-TYPE-CD
MOVE DALYTRAN-CAT-CD TO TRAN-CAT-CD
MOVE DALYTRAN-SOURCE TO TRAN-SOURCE
MOVE DALYTRAN-DESC TO TRAN-DESC

Detailed_Business_Rule: Dispatches the three downstream updates in fixed order: first update the transaction-category-balance file (read-modify-write or create), then update the account record's running balance and cycle credit/debit, then write the fully-populated transaction record to the master transaction file.
SQL_Decision_Control_Statements: PERFORM 2700-UPDATE-TCATBAL\nPERFORM 2800-UPDATE-ACCOUNT-REC\nPERFORM 2900-WRITE-TRANSACTION-FILE

Input_File_Layout: DALYTRAN-ID(PIC X(16))|DALYTRAN-TYPE-CD(PIC X(02))|DALYTRAN-CAT-CD(PIC 9(04))|DALYTRAN-SOURCE(PIC X(10))|DALYTRAN-DESC(PIC X(100))|DALYTRAN-AMT(PIC S9(09)V99)|DALYTRAN-MERCHANT-ID(PIC 9(09))|DALYTRAN-MERCHANT-NAME(PIC X(50))|DALYTRAN-MERCHANT-CITY(PIC X(50))|DALYTRAN-MERCHANT-ZIP(PIC X(10))|DALYTRAN-CARD-NUM(PIC X(16))|DALYTRAN-ORIG-TS(PIC X(26))|DALYTRAN-PROC-TS(PIC X(26))|FILLER(PIC X(20))
Detailed_Business_Rule: Writes a 430-byte reject record to the daily rejects file. The reject record contains the complete original 350-byte daily transaction followed by a 4-byte reason code and a 76-byte reason description trailer. A non-zero file status displays an I/O error and abends the program with completion code 999.
SQL_Decision_Control_Statements:
MOVE DALYTRAN-RECORD TO REJECT-TRAN-DATA
MOVE WS-VALIDATION-TRAILER TO VALIDATION-TRAILER
WRITE FD-REJS-RECORD FROM REJECT-RECORD
IF DALYREJS-STATUS = '00'
    MOVE 0 TO APPL-RESULT

Detailed_Business_Rule: Rewrites the account record with the updated balance and cycle credit/debit. If the rewrite hits an INVALID KEY path (the underlying account vanished between read and rewrite, which is unusual) the validation reason code is set to 109 with description 'ACCOUNT RECORD NOT FOUND'. Note that this reject code 109 is set inside the posting paragraph (after the validation block has already passed) so the calling code does not actually use it as a rejection trigger - it is captured here for diagnostic purposes.
SQL_Decision_Control_Statements:
REWRITE FD-ACCTFILE-REC FROM ACCOUNT-RECORD
    INVALID KEY
        MOVE 109 TO WS-VALIDATION-FAIL-REASON
        MOVE 'ACCOUNT RECORD NOT FOUND' TO WS-VALIDATION-FAIL-REASON-DESC
END-REWRITE
Review_Comments: Reject code 109 is set after main validation block has cleared; it does NOT cause the original transaction to be re-routed to the rejects file. This is a partial-failure silent path that should be surfaced via a status field in the modernized service.

Detailed_Business_Rule: Builds a 26-character DB2-format timestamp YYYY-MM-DD-HH.MM.SS.ssssss from the COBOL intrinsic FUNCTION CURRENT-DATE result, inserting hyphens between YYYY/MM/DD and between DD and HH and dots between HH/MM/SS/ssssss. The trailing four characters are zero-filled.
SQL_Decision_Control_Statements:
MOVE FUNCTION CURRENT-DATE TO COBOL-TS
MOVE COB-YYYY TO DB2-YYYY
MOVE COB-MM   TO DB2-MM
MOVE COB-DD   TO DB2-DD
MOVE COB-HH   TO DB2-HH
MOVE COB-MIN  TO DB2-MIN
MOVE COB-SS   TO DB2-SS
MOVE COB-MIL  TO DB2-MIL
MOVE '0000' TO DB2-REST
MOVE '-' TO DB2-STREEP-1 DB2-STREEP-2 DB2-STREEP-3
MOVE '.' TO DB2-DOT-1 DB2-DOT-2 DB2-DOT-3
Review_Comments: Replace with java.time.LocalDateTime.now() and DateTimeFormatter.ofPattern in Java migration.

Detailed_Business_Rule: Universal abend routine. Sets timing to 0 and abend code to 999 then calls the Language Environment service CEE3ABD which terminates the COBOL run unit and produces a U0999 system completion code visible in JES output.
SQL_Decision_Control_Statements:
DISPLAY 'ABENDING PROGRAM'
MOVE 0 TO TIMING
MOVE 999 TO ABCODE
CALL 'CEE3ABD'
Review_Comments: Hardcoded magic value: ABCODE 999. Replace with structured exception in Java (RuntimeException with error context).

Detailed_Business_Rule: Diagnostic helper that converts the 2-byte COBOL file-status code into a 4-character display form. When the first byte is 9 the second byte holds a binary value that is expanded to its decimal representation; otherwise the 2-byte numeric status is padded with leading zeros to four characters.
SQL_Decision_Control_Statements:
IF IO-STATUS NOT NUMERIC OR IO-STAT1 = '9'
    MOVE IO-STAT1 TO IO-STATUS-04(1:1)
    MOVE 0 TO TWO-BYTES-BINARY
    MOVE IO-STAT2 TO TWO-BYTES-RIGHT
    MOVE TWO-BYTES-BINARY TO IO-STATUS-0403
    DISPLAY 'FILE STATUS IS: NNNN' IO-STATUS-04
ELSE
    MOVE '0000' TO IO-STATUS-04
    MOVE IO-STATUS TO IO-STATUS-04(3:2)
    DISPLAY 'FILE STATUS IS: NNNN' IO-STATUS-04
END-IF

Detailed_Business_Rule: Opens the new system-generated transaction sequential output file for OUTPUT. Each interest amount computed during the run is appended as a new TRAN-RECORD with a synthetic transaction id assembled from the PARM date plus a 6-digit suffix.
SQL_Decision_Control_Statements:
OPEN OUTPUT TRANSACT-FILE
IF TRANFILE-STATUS = '00'
    MOVE 0 TO APPL-RESULT
ELSE
    PERFORM 9999-ABEND-PROGRAM

Detailed_Business_Rule: Reads the next sequential record from the transaction-category-balance file. File status 00 is success; status 10 marks end-of-file and sets the END-OF-FILE flag to Y so the main loop drives one final 1050-UPDATE-ACCOUNT call to flush the last account; any other status displays an I/O error and abends the program with completion code 999.
SQL_Decision_Control_Statements:
READ TCATBAL-FILE INTO TRAN-CAT-BAL-RECORD
IF TCATBALF-STATUS = '00'
    MOVE 0 TO APPL-RESULT
ELSE IF TCATBALF-STATUS = '10'
    MOVE 16 TO APPL-RESULT
ELSE
    MOVE 12 TO APPL-RESULT
IF APPL-EOF
    MOVE 'Y' TO END-OF-FILE
ELSE
    PERFORM 9999-ABEND-PROGRAM

Detailed_Business_Rule: Increments the record counter for every successfully read TCATBAL row and displays the read row to SYSOUT for trace.
SQL_Decision_Control_Statements: ADD 1 TO WS-RECORD-COUNT\nDISPLAY TRAN-CAT-BAL-RECORD

Detailed_Business_Rule: Detects when the account ID just read differs from the previously processed account number. On a change the prior account's accumulated interest is committed via 1050-UPDATE-ACCOUNT (skipped on the very first iteration via the WS-FIRST-TIME flag), the running interest accumulator is reset to zero, and the new account's master and cross-reference records are loaded into memory.
SQL_Decision_Control_Statements:
IF TRANCAT-ACCT-ID NOT= WS-LAST-ACCT-NUM
    IF WS-FIRST-TIME NOT = 'Y'
        PERFORM 1050-UPDATE-ACCOUNT
    ELSE
        MOVE 'N' TO WS-FIRST-TIME
    END-IF
    MOVE 0 TO WS-TOTAL-INT
    MOVE TRANCAT-ACCT-ID TO WS-LAST-ACCT-NUM
    MOVE TRANCAT-ACCT-ID TO FD-ACCT-ID
    PERFORM 1100-GET-ACCT-DATA
    MOVE TRANCAT-ACCT-ID TO FD-XREF-ACCT-ID
    PERFORM 1110-GET-XREF-DATA
END-IF

Input_File_Layout: ACCT-ID(PIC 9(11))|ACCT-ACTIVE-STATUS(PIC X(01))|ACCT-CURR-BAL(PIC S9(10)V99)|ACCT-CREDIT-LIMIT(PIC S9(10)V99)|ACCT-CASH-CREDIT-LIMIT(PIC S9(10)V99)|ACCT-OPEN-DATE(PIC X(10))|ACCT-EXPIRAION-DATE(PIC X(10))|ACCT-REISSUE-DATE(PIC X(10))|ACCT-CURR-CYC-CREDIT(PIC S9(10)V99)|ACCT-CURR-CYC-DEBIT(PIC S9(10)V99)|ACCT-ADDR-ZIP(PIC X(10))|ACCT-GROUP-ID(PIC X(10))|FILLER(PIC X(178))|TRANCAT-ACCT-ID(PIC 9(11))|TRANCAT-TYPE-CD(PIC X(02))|TRANCAT-CD(PIC 9(04))|TRAN-CAT-BAL(PIC S9(09)V99)|FILLER(PIC X(22))
Linkage_Columns: ACCT-GROUP-ID + TRANCAT-CD + TRANCAT-TYPE-CD -> DISCGRP composite key
Detailed_Business_Rule: Builds the disclosure-group lookup key from three parts: the account's group id (10 characters), the current transaction category (4 digits), and the current transaction type (2 characters).
SQL_Decision_Control_Statements: MOVE ACCT-GROUP-ID TO FD-DIS-ACCT-GROUP-ID\nMOVE TRANCAT-CD TO FD-DIS-TRAN-CAT-CD\nMOVE TRANCAT-TYPE-CD TO FD-DIS-TRAN-TYPE-CD

Detailed_Business_Rule: Skips both the interest computation and the fee computation when the looked-up annual disclosure interest rate equals zero. Zero-rate categories generate no interest postings.
SQL_Decision_Control_Statements:
PERFORM 1200-GET-INTEREST-RATE
IF DIS-INT-RATE NOT = 0
    PERFORM 1300-COMPUTE-INTEREST
    PERFORM 1400-COMPUTE-FEES
END-IF

Output_File_Layout: ACCT-ID(PIC 9(11))|ACCT-ACTIVE-STATUS(PIC X(01))|ACCT-CURR-BAL(PIC S9(10)V99)|ACCT-CREDIT-LIMIT(PIC S9(10)V99)|ACCT-CASH-CREDIT-LIMIT(PIC S9(10)V99)|ACCT-OPEN-DATE(PIC X(10))|ACCT-EXPIRAION-DATE(PIC X(10))|ACCT-REISSUE-DATE(PIC X(10))|ACCT-CURR-CYC-CREDIT(PIC S9(10)V99)|ACCT-CURR-CYC-DEBIT(PIC S9(10)V99)|ACCT-ADDR-ZIP(PIC X(10))|ACCT-GROUP-ID(PIC X(10))|FILLER(PIC X(178))
Detailed_Business_Rule: On end-of-file the main loop performs one final 1050-UPDATE-ACCOUNT to flush the last account's accumulated interest. Without this branch the last account in the file would never have its interest committed.
SQL_Decision_Control_Statements: ELSE\n     PERFORM 1050-UPDATE-ACCOUNT

Input_File_Layout: ACCT-ID(PIC 9(11))|ACCT-ACTIVE-STATUS(PIC X(01))|ACCT-CURR-BAL(PIC S9(10)V99)|ACCT-CREDIT-LIMIT(PIC S9(10)V99)|ACCT-CASH-CREDIT-LIMIT(PIC S9(10)V99)|ACCT-OPEN-DATE(PIC X(10))|ACCT-EXPIRAION-DATE(PIC X(10))|ACCT-REISSUE-DATE(PIC X(10))|ACCT-CURR-CYC-CREDIT(PIC S9(10)V99)|ACCT-CURR-CYC-DEBIT(PIC S9(10)V99)|ACCT-ADDR-ZIP(PIC X(10))|ACCT-GROUP-ID(PIC X(10))|FILLER(PIC X(178))
Output_File_Layout: ACCT-ID(PIC 9(11))|ACCT-ACTIVE-STATUS(PIC X(01))|ACCT-CURR-BAL(PIC S9(10)V99)|ACCT-CREDIT-LIMIT(PIC S9(10)V99)|ACCT-CASH-CREDIT-LIMIT(PIC S9(10)V99)|ACCT-OPEN-DATE(PIC X(10))|ACCT-EXPIRAION-DATE(PIC X(10))|ACCT-REISSUE-DATE(PIC X(10))|ACCT-CURR-CYC-CREDIT(PIC S9(10)V99)|ACCT-CURR-CYC-DEBIT(PIC S9(10)V99)|ACCT-ADDR-ZIP(PIC X(10))|ACCT-GROUP-ID(PIC X(10))|FILLER(PIC X(178))
Detailed_Business_Rule: Adds the accumulated total monthly interest for the current account to the account's running balance.
SQL_Decision_Control_Statements: ADD WS-TOTAL-INT TO ACCT-CURR-BAL
Review_Comments: Packed-decimal COMP-3 arithmetic; map to BigDecimal in Java migration.

Output_File_Layout: ACCT-ID(PIC 9(11))|ACCT-ACTIVE-STATUS(PIC X(01))|ACCT-CURR-BAL(PIC S9(10)V99)|ACCT-CREDIT-LIMIT(PIC S9(10)V99)|ACCT-CASH-CREDIT-LIMIT(PIC S9(10)V99)|ACCT-OPEN-DATE(PIC X(10))|ACCT-EXPIRAION-DATE(PIC X(10))|ACCT-REISSUE-DATE(PIC X(10))|ACCT-CURR-CYC-CREDIT(PIC S9(10)V99)|ACCT-CURR-CYC-DEBIT(PIC S9(10)V99)|ACCT-ADDR-ZIP(PIC X(10))|ACCT-GROUP-ID(PIC X(10))|FILLER(PIC X(178))
Detailed_Business_Rule: Resets the current cycle's credit and debit accumulators to zero after interest is posted. This means after the interest run the account starts a fresh statement cycle with zero accumulated cycle credit and zero accumulated cycle debit. The next daily transaction posting run will rebuild the cycle accumulators from scratch.
SQL_Decision_Control_Statements: MOVE 0 TO ACCT-CURR-CYC-CREDIT\nMOVE 0 TO ACCT-CURR-CYC-DEBIT
Review_Comments: Inferred business semantics: interest posting marks end-of-statement-cycle. Document this dependency explicitly in the modernized service contract.

Input_File_Layout: ACCT-ID(PIC 9(11))|ACCT-ACTIVE-STATUS(PIC X(01))|ACCT-CURR-BAL(PIC S9(10)V99)|ACCT-CREDIT-LIMIT(PIC S9(10)V99)|ACCT-CASH-CREDIT-LIMIT(PIC S9(10)V99)|ACCT-OPEN-DATE(PIC X(10))|ACCT-EXPIRAION-DATE(PIC X(10))|ACCT-REISSUE-DATE(PIC X(10))|ACCT-CURR-CYC-CREDIT(PIC S9(10)V99)|ACCT-CURR-CYC-DEBIT(PIC S9(10)V99)|ACCT-ADDR-ZIP(PIC X(10))|ACCT-GROUP-ID(PIC X(10))|FILLER(PIC X(178))
Output_File_Layout: ACCT-ID(PIC 9(11))|ACCT-ACTIVE-STATUS(PIC X(01))|ACCT-CURR-BAL(PIC S9(10)V99)|ACCT-CREDIT-LIMIT(PIC S9(10)V99)|ACCT-CASH-CREDIT-LIMIT(PIC S9(10)V99)|ACCT-OPEN-DATE(PIC X(10))|ACCT-EXPIRAION-DATE(PIC X(10))|ACCT-REISSUE-DATE(PIC X(10))|ACCT-CURR-CYC-CREDIT(PIC S9(10)V99)|ACCT-CURR-CYC-DEBIT(PIC S9(10)V99)|ACCT-ADDR-ZIP(PIC X(10))|ACCT-GROUP-ID(PIC X(10))|FILLER(PIC X(178))
Detailed_Business_Rule: Rewrites the account record with the updated balance and the cleared cycle credit/debit. A non-zero file status terminates the program with completion code 999.
SQL_Decision_Control_Statements:
REWRITE FD-ACCTFILE-REC FROM ACCOUNT-RECORD
IF ACCTFILE-STATUS = '00'
    MOVE 0 TO APPL-RESULT
ELSE
    DISPLAY 'ERROR RE-WRITING ACCOUNT FILE'
    PERFORM 9999-ABEND-PROGRAM

Input_File_Layout: ACCT-ID(PIC 9(11))|ACCT-ACTIVE-STATUS(PIC X(01))|ACCT-CURR-BAL(PIC S9(10)V99)|ACCT-CREDIT-LIMIT(PIC S9(10)V99)|ACCT-CASH-CREDIT-LIMIT(PIC S9(10)V99)|ACCT-OPEN-DATE(PIC X(10))|ACCT-EXPIRAION-DATE(PIC X(10))|ACCT-REISSUE-DATE(PIC X(10))|ACCT-CURR-CYC-CREDIT(PIC S9(10)V99)|ACCT-CURR-CYC-DEBIT(PIC S9(10)V99)|ACCT-ADDR-ZIP(PIC X(10))|ACCT-GROUP-ID(PIC X(10))|FILLER(PIC X(178))
Detailed_Business_Rule: Reads the account master by the primary key (account ID) using random access. INVALID KEY simply displays a diagnostic 'ACCOUNT NOT FOUND' but the file status check that follows then aborts the program with completion code 999, so the diagnostic display is purely informational and the program does not gracefully recover from a missing account.
SQL_Decision_Control_Statements:
READ ACCOUNT-FILE INTO ACCOUNT-RECORD
    INVALID KEY
        DISPLAY 'ACCOUNT NOT FOUND: ' FD-ACCT-ID
END-READ
IF ACCTFILE-STATUS = '00'
    MOVE 0 TO APPL-RESULT
ELSE
    PERFORM 9999-ABEND-PROGRAM
Review_Comments: Silent failure: INVALID KEY only displays a message; the abend in the next IF block is the actual control mechanism. Refactor to throw an exception on missing account in modernized service.

Detailed_Business_Rule: Reads the card cross-reference by the alternate key (account ID) so that the card number associated with the account can later be embedded in the synthetic interest transaction. The XREFFIL1 DD provides the alternate-index path.
SQL_Decision_Control_Statements:
READ XREF-FILE INTO CARD-XREF-RECORD
 KEY IS FD-XREF-ACCT-ID
    INVALID KEY
        DISPLAY 'ACCOUNT NOT FOUND: ' FD-XREF-ACCT-ID
END-READ
IF XREFFILE-STATUS = '00'
    MOVE 0 TO APPL-RESULT
ELSE
    PERFORM 9999-ABEND-PROGRAM
Review_Comments: Silent failure: INVALID KEY only displays a message. Replace VSAM alternate-index pattern with PostgreSQL non-unique index (account_id) on a card_xref table.

Linkage_Columns: ACCT-GROUP-ID + TRANCAT-CD + TRANCAT-TYPE-CD -> DISCGRP composite key
Detailed_Business_Rule: Reads the disclosure group rate file by the 16-byte composite key (account group, transaction type, transaction category). INVALID KEY displays diagnostics and falls through to the file status check.
SQL_Decision_Control_Statements:
READ DISCGRP-FILE INTO DIS-GROUP-RECORD
    INVALID KEY
        DISPLAY 'DISCLOSURE GROUP RECORD MISSING'
        DISPLAY 'TRY WITH DEFAULT GROUP CODE'
END-READ

Detailed_Business_Rule: Treats both file-status 00 (record found) and 23 (record not found) as success. The 23 case is then dispatched to the default-rate fallback paragraph. Any other status is a real I/O error that aborts the program.
SQL_Decision_Control_Statements:
IF DISCGRP-STATUS = '00'  OR '23'
    MOVE 0 TO APPL-RESULT
ELSE
    MOVE 12 TO APPL-RESULT
    PERFORM 9999-ABEND-PROGRAM
END-IF

Detailed_Business_Rule: When the original disclosure-group lookup returned 'record not found', substitutes the literal 'DEFAULT' as the account-group-id and re-reads the disclosure rate file. This guarantees a fallback rate exists for any unknown account group.
SQL_Decision_Control_Statements:
IF DISCGRP-STATUS = '23'
    MOVE 'DEFAULT' TO FD-DIS-ACCT-GROUP-ID
    PERFORM 1200-A-GET-DEFAULT-INT-RATE
END-IF
Review_Comments: Hardcoded magic value: 'DEFAULT' literal as the universal fallback group key.

Detailed_Business_Rule: Re-reads the disclosure-group rate file using the substituted 'DEFAULT' group key. A non-zero file status (e.g. the DEFAULT row is missing entirely) terminates the program with completion code 999 because no fallback is available.
SQL_Decision_Control_Statements:
READ DISCGRP-FILE INTO DIS-GROUP-RECORD
IF DISCGRP-STATUS = '00'
    MOVE 0 TO APPL-RESULT
ELSE
    DISPLAY 'ERROR READING DEFAULT DISCLOSURE GROUP'
    PERFORM 9999-ABEND-PROGRAM

Input_File_Layout: TRANCAT-ACCT-ID(PIC 9(11))|TRANCAT-TYPE-CD(PIC X(02))|TRANCAT-CD(PIC 9(04))|TRAN-CAT-BAL(PIC S9(09)V99)|FILLER(PIC X(22))|DIS-ACCT-GROUP-ID(PIC X(10))|DIS-TRAN-TYPE-CD(PIC X(02))|DIS-TRAN-CAT-CD(PIC 9(04))|DIS-INT-RATE(PIC S9(04)V99)|FILLER(PIC X(28))
Detailed_Business_Rule: Computes the monthly interest amount as the category balance multiplied by the annual disclosure rate divided by 1200. The constant 1200 equals 100 (percent-to-fraction) times 12 (annual-to-monthly), giving a single divisor that converts a percentage annual rate into a fractional monthly multiplier.
SQL_Decision_Control_Statements: COMPUTE WS-MONTHLY-INT\n    = ( TRAN-CAT-BAL * DIS-INT-RATE) / 1200
Review_Comments: Hardcoded magic value: divisor 1200 encodes the percent-to-monthly conversion factor (100 * 12). Document as a named constant in the modernized service.

Detailed_Business_Rule: Adds this category's monthly interest amount to the running per-account total interest accumulator before dispatching to the transaction-write paragraph.
SQL_Decision_Control_Statements: ADD WS-MONTHLY-INT TO WS-TOTAL-INT\nPERFORM 1300-B-WRITE-TX

Output_File_Layout: TRAN-ID(PIC X(16))|TRAN-TYPE-CD(PIC X(02))|TRAN-CAT-CD(PIC 9(04))|TRAN-SOURCE(PIC X(10))|TRAN-DESC(PIC X(100))|TRAN-AMT(PIC S9(09)V99)|TRAN-MERCHANT-ID(PIC 9(09))|TRAN-MERCHANT-NAME(PIC X(50))|TRAN-MERCHANT-CITY(PIC X(50))|TRAN-MERCHANT-ZIP(PIC X(10))|TRAN-CARD-NUM(PIC X(16))|TRAN-ORIG-TS(PIC X(26))|TRAN-PROC-TS(PIC X(26))|FILLER(PIC X(20))
Detailed_Business_Rule: Builds a unique 16-character synthetic transaction ID by concatenating the 10-character PARM-DATE and a 6-digit zero-padded counter that increments by one for every interest transaction generated during the run.
SQL_Decision_Control_Statements:
ADD 1 TO WS-TRANID-SUFFIX
STRING PARM-DATE,
       WS-TRANID-SUFFIX
  DELIMITED BY SIZE
  INTO TRAN-ID
END-STRING

Output_File_Layout: TRAN-ID(PIC X(16))|TRAN-TYPE-CD(PIC X(02))|TRAN-CAT-CD(PIC 9(04))|TRAN-SOURCE(PIC X(10))|TRAN-DESC(PIC X(100))|TRAN-AMT(PIC S9(09)V99)|TRAN-MERCHANT-ID(PIC 9(09))|TRAN-MERCHANT-NAME(PIC X(50))|TRAN-MERCHANT-CITY(PIC X(50))|TRAN-MERCHANT-ZIP(PIC X(10))|TRAN-CARD-NUM(PIC X(16))|TRAN-ORIG-TS(PIC X(26))|TRAN-PROC-TS(PIC X(26))|FILLER(PIC X(20))
Detailed_Business_Rule: Populates the synthetic interest transaction with hardcoded values: transaction type '01' (the conventional credit type code), transaction category '0005' (the conventional interest category code), source 'System' (so downstream reports can distinguish system-generated interest from card-swipe transactions), description 'Int. for a/c <id>' (human-readable). The transaction amount is the previously computed monthly interest, the merchant fields are blank, the card number is taken from the alternate-key cross-reference lookup, and both origination and processing timestamps are set to the current DB2-format timestamp.
SQL_Decision_Control_Statements:
MOVE '01' TO TRAN-TYPE-CD
MOVE '05' TO TRAN-CAT-CD
MOVE 'System' TO TRAN-SOURCE
STRING 'Int. for a/c ',
       ACCT-ID DELIMITED BY SIZE
       INTO TRAN-DESC
END-STRING
MOVE WS-MONTHLY-INT TO TRAN-AMT
MOVE 0 TO TRAN-MERCHANT-ID
MOVE SPACES TO TRAN-MERCHANT-NAME
MOVE SPACES TO TRAN-MERCHANT-CITY
MOVE SPACES TO TRAN-MERCHANT-ZIP
MOVE XREF-CARD-NUM TO TRAN-CARD-NUM
PERFORM Z-GET-DB2-FORMAT-TIMESTAMP
MOVE DB2-FORMAT-TS TO TRAN-ORIG-TS
MOVE DB2-FORMAT-TS TO TRAN-PROC-TS
Review_Comments: Hardcoded magic value: TRAN-TYPE-CD '01', TRAN-CAT-CD '05', TRAN-SOURCE 'System', TRAN-DESC prefix 'Int. for a/c '. Replace with reference data table or enum in the modernized service so code changes are not required to add new system-generated transaction kinds.

Output_File_Layout: TRAN-ID(PIC X(16))|TRAN-TYPE-CD(PIC X(02))|TRAN-CAT-CD(PIC 9(04))|TRAN-SOURCE(PIC X(10))|TRAN-DESC(PIC X(100))|TRAN-AMT(PIC S9(09)V99)|TRAN-MERCHANT-ID(PIC 9(09))|TRAN-MERCHANT-NAME(PIC X(50))|TRAN-MERCHANT-CITY(PIC X(50))|TRAN-MERCHANT-ZIP(PIC X(10))|TRAN-CARD-NUM(PIC X(16))|TRAN-ORIG-TS(PIC X(26))|TRAN-PROC-TS(PIC X(26))|FILLER(PIC X(20))
Detailed_Business_Rule: Writes the populated interest transaction record to the SYSTRAN sequential output dataset. A non-zero file status terminates the program with completion code 999.
SQL_Decision_Control_Statements:
WRITE FD-TRANFILE-REC FROM TRAN-RECORD
IF TRANFILE-STATUS = '00'
    MOVE 0 TO APPL-RESULT
ELSE
    DISPLAY 'ERROR WRITING TRANSACTION RECORD'
    PERFORM 9999-ABEND-PROGRAM

Detailed_Business_Rule: Empty stub paragraph documented as 'To be implemented'. The main-loop dispatcher calls it after 1300-COMPUTE-INTEREST when the disclosure rate is non-zero, but the body consists of a single EXIT statement so no fees are ever computed today.
SQL_Decision_Control_Statements: 1400-COMPUTE-FEES.\n* To be implemented\n    EXIT
Review_Comments: Dead code: 1400-COMPUTE-FEES paragraph is an empty stub. Either remove the call site or implement the planned fee computation in the modernized service.

Detailed_Business_Rule: Closes the transaction-category-balance VSAM KSDS. A non-zero close status terminates the program with completion code 999.

Detailed_Business_Rule: Closes the card cross-reference VSAM KSDS. A non-zero close status terminates the program with completion code 999.
SQL_Decision_Control_Statements: CLOSE XREF-FILE

Detailed_Business_Rule: Closes the disclosure-group rate VSAM KSDS. A non-zero close status terminates the program with completion code 999.

Output_File_Layout: TRAN-ID(PIC X(16))|TRAN-TYPE-CD(PIC X(02))|TRAN-CAT-CD(PIC 9(04))|TRAN-SOURCE(PIC X(10))|TRAN-DESC(PIC X(100))|TRAN-AMT(PIC S9(09)V99)|TRAN-MERCHANT-ID(PIC 9(09))|TRAN-MERCHANT-NAME(PIC X(50))|TRAN-MERCHANT-CITY(PIC X(50))|TRAN-MERCHANT-ZIP(PIC X(10))|TRAN-CARD-NUM(PIC X(16))|TRAN-ORIG-TS(PIC X(26))|TRAN-PROC-TS(PIC X(26))|FILLER(PIC X(20))

SQL_Decision_Control_Statements: CLOSE TRANSACT-FILE

Detailed_Business_Rule: Returns control to the JCL job step.
SQL_Decision_Control_Statements: GOBACK

Detailed_Business_Rule: Builds a 26-character DB2-format timestamp from the COBOL intrinsic FUNCTION CURRENT-DATE result, used as both the origination and processing timestamp on every system-generated interest transaction.
SQL_Decision_Control_Statements:
MOVE FUNCTION CURRENT-DATE TO COBOL-TS
MOVE COB-YYYY TO DB2-YYYY
MOVE COB-MM   TO DB2-MM
MOVE COB-DD   TO DB2-DD
MOVE COB-HH   TO DB2-HH
MOVE COB-MIN  TO DB2-MIN
MOVE COB-SS   TO DB2-SS
MOVE COB-MIL  TO DB2-MIL
MOVE '0000' TO DB2-REST
MOVE '-' TO DB2-STREEP-1 DB2-STREEP-2 DB2-STREEP-3
MOVE '.' TO DB2-DOT-1 DB2-DOT-2 DB2-DOT-3
Review_Comments: Replace with java.time.LocalDateTime.now() and DateTimeFormatter.ofPattern in Java migration.

Detailed_Business_Rule: Universal abend routine. Sets timing to 0 and abend code to 999 then calls the Language Environment service CEE3ABD which terminates the COBOL run unit and produces a U0999 system completion code.
SQL_Decision_Control_Statements:
DISPLAY 'ABENDING PROGRAM'
MOVE 0 TO TIMING
MOVE 999 TO ABCODE
CALL 'CEE3ABD'
Review_Comments: Hardcoded magic value: ABCODE 999. Replace with structured exception in Java migration.

Detailed_Business_Rule: Diagnostic helper that converts the 2-byte COBOL file-status code into a 4-character display form for SYSOUT trace messages.
SQL_Decision_Control_Statements:
IF IO-STATUS NOT NUMERIC OR IO-STAT1 = '9'
    MOVE IO-STAT1 TO IO-STATUS-04(1:1)
    MOVE 0 TO TWO-BYTES-BINARY
    MOVE IO-STAT2 TO TWO-BYTES-RIGHT
    MOVE TWO-BYTES-BINARY TO IO-STATUS-0403
ELSE
    MOVE '0000' TO IO-STATUS-04
    MOVE IO-STATUS TO IO-STATUS-04(3:2)
END-IF

Input_File_Layout: TRAN-ID(PIC X(16))|TRAN-TYPE-CD(PIC X(02))|TRAN-CAT-CD(PIC 9(04))|TRAN-SOURCE(PIC X(10))|TRAN-DESC(PIC X(100))|TRAN-AMT(PIC S9(09)V99)|TRAN-MERCHANT-ID(PIC 9(09))|TRAN-MERCHANT-NAME(PIC X(50))|TRAN-MERCHANT-CITY(PIC X(50))|TRAN-MERCHANT-ZIP(PIC X(10))|TRAN-CARD-NUM(PIC X(16))|TRAN-ORIG-TS(PIC X(26))|TRAN-PROC-TS(PIC X(26))|FILLER(PIC X(20))

Detailed_Business_Rule: Concatenates the prior backed-up master transaction file (TRANSACT.BKUP generation 0, the latest existing backup) and the system-generated interest transaction file (SYSTRAN generation 0) and sorts the resulting 350-byte fixed-length records ascending by the 16-character transaction ID at offset 1.     MOVE 0 TO APPL-RESULT
ELSE
    PERFORM 9999-ABEND-PROGRAM

Detailed_Business_Rule: Reads one record from the date-parameter file and dispatches on the file status: 00 echoes the report range to SYSOUT (Reporting from <start> to <end>); 10 (end-of-file after a single read attempt with no data) sets the END-OF-FILE flag to Y; any other status displays an I/O error and abends with completion code 999.
SQL_Decision_Control_Statements:
READ DATE-PARMS-FILE INTO WS-DATEPARM-RECORD
EVALUATE DATEPARM-STATUS
  WHEN '00'
      MOVE 0 TO APPL-RESULT
  WHEN '10'
      MOVE 16 TO APPL-RESULT
  WHEN OTHER
      MOVE 12 TO APPL-RESULT
END-EVALUATE
IF APPL-AOK
    DISPLAY 'Reporting from ' WS-START-DATE ' to ' WS-END-DATE
ELSE IF APPL-EOF
    MOVE 'Y' TO END-OF-FILE
ELSE
    PERFORM 9999-ABEND-PROGRAM

Detailed_Business_Rule: Diagnostic helper that converts the 2-byte COBOL file-status code into a 4-character display form for SYSOUT trace.
SQL_Decision_Control_Statements:
IF IO-STATUS NOT NUMERIC OR IO-STAT1 = '9'
    MOVE IO-STAT1 TO IO-STATUS-04(1:1)
    MOVE 0 TO TWO-BYTES-BINARY
    MOVE IO-STAT2 TO TWO-BYTES-RIGHT
    MOVE TWO-BYTES-BINARY TO IO-STATUS-0403
ELSE
    MOVE '0000' TO IO-STATUS-04
    MOVE IO-STATUS TO IO-STATUS-04(3:2)
END-IF

Detailed_Business_Rule: Deletes any existing TRXFL sequential and VSAM KSDS clusters from a prior run. The SET MAXCC = 0 statement suppresses non-zero return codes from missing-dataset DELETE attempts so the step succeeds on a fresh environment.
SQL_Decision_Control_Statements:
DELETE    AWS.M2.CARDDEMO.TRXFL.SEQ
DELETE    AWS.M2.CARDDEMO.TRXFL.VSAM.KSDS                     -
          CLUSTER
SET       MAXCC = 0
Review_Comments: Hardcoded DSN: AWS.M2.CARDDEMO.TRXFL.SEQ, AWS.M2.CARDDEMO.TRXFL.VSAM.KSDS. Replace with idempotent DROP TABLE IF EXISTS in PostgreSQL or with S3 prefix delete in AWS Glue migration.

Output_File_Layout: TRNX-CARD-NUM(PIC X(16))|TRNX-ID(PIC X(16))|TRNX-TYPE-CD(PIC X(02))|TRNX-CAT-CD(PIC 9(04))|TRNX-SOURCE(PIC X(10))|TRNX-DESC(PIC X(100))|TRNX-AMT(PIC S9(09)V99)|TRNX-MERCHANT-ID(PIC 9(09))|TRNX-MERCHANT-NAME(PIC X(50))|TRNX-MERCHANT-CITY(PIC X(50))|TRNX-MERCHANT-ZIP(PIC X(10))|TRNX-ORIG-TS(PIC X(26))|TRNX-PROC-TS(PIC X(26))|FILLER(PIC X(20))
Detailed_Business_Rule: Defines a fresh VSAM KSDS cluster TRXFL with key length 32 starting at offset 0 (the concatenated 16-byte card number plus 16-byte transaction id), a fixed record size of 350 bytes, share-options 2 3 (one writer or many readers, full integrity across regions), and a primary 1-cylinder data area with 5 secondary cylinders. The DATA and INDEX components are named explicitly with a control-interval size of 4096.
SQL_Decision_Control_Statements:
DEFINE    CLUSTER  (NAME(AWS.M2.CARDDEMO.TRXFL.VSAM.KSDS)     -
                    KEYS(32 0)                                -
                    VOLUMES(TSU023)                           -
                    RECORDSIZE(350 350)                       -
                    SHAREOPTIONS(2 3)                         -
                    ERASE                                     -
                    INDEXED                                   -
                    CYL(1 5))                                 -
          DATA      (NAME(AWS.M2.CARDDEMO.TRXFL.DATA)         -
                    CISZ(4096))                               -
          INDEX     (NAME(AWS.M2.CARDDEMO.TRXFL.INDEX))
Review_Comments: Hardcoded DSN, hardcoded VOLUMES(TSU023). Replace VSAM KSDS with PostgreSQL table primary key (card_num, tran_id) and DROP/CREATE in a Liquibase changelog or Spring Batch tasklet.

Detailed_Business_Rule: Sorts the master transaction file ascending first by 16-character card number at offset 263 then by 16-character transaction id at offset 1. The OUTREC reformats the output so the new layout begins with the card number followed by the original first 262 bytes followed by the original 50-byte tail. The output dataset is the staging sequential file that will be REPRO'd into the TRXFL VSAM KSDS in the next step.
Review_Comments: SORT utility step. Replace with Spring Batch JdbcCursorItemReader with ORDER BY card_num, tran_id; replace with DataFrame.orderBy(['card_num','tran_id']).select(...) in Glue migration.

Detailed_Business_Rule: Loads the sorted sequential file into the freshly defined TRXFL VSAM KSDS via IDCAMS REPRO. The COND=(0,NE) guard ensures the step is skipped if any prior step set a non-zero return code so a half-built KSDS is not produced.
Review_Comments: IDCAMS REPRO bulk-load. Replace with PostgreSQL COPY in Java migration.

Detailed_Business_Rule: IEFBR14 is the no-op program used to apply DD-statement disposition processing. The DISP=(MOD,DELETE,DELETE) on both HTMLFILE and STMTFILE means the dataset is allocated and immediately deleted, providing an idempotent way to remove prior-run report files before the statement creator step recreates them. The COND=(0,NE) guard skips the step on prior failure.
SQL_Decision_Control_Statements: //STEP030 EXEC PGM=IEFBR14,COND=(0,NE)\n//HTMLFILE DD DISP=(MOD,DELETE,DELETE),DSN=AWS.M2.CARDDEMO.STATEMNT.HTML\n//STMTFILE DD DISP=(MOD,DELETE,DELETE),DSN=AWS.M2.CARDDEMO.STATEMNT.PS
Review_Comments: IEFBR14 cleanup with DISP=(MOD,DELETE,DELETE) trick. Replace with explicit S3 delete or Spring Batch JobExecutionListener.beforeJob() cleanup hook.

Detailed_Business_Rule: Executes the statement creator program. The job allocates the freshly-loaded TRXFL VSAM KSDS as TRNXFILE (the per-card transaction work file) and three master VSAM files (XREFFILE, ACCTFILE, CUSTFILE), and creates two new output GDG datasets: STMTFILE (80-byte text) and HTMLFILE (100-byte HTML). The COND=(0,NE) guard skips the step on prior failure.
Review_Comments: Hardcoded DSN for 6 datasets. Replace VSAM with PostgreSQL/DynamoDB and PS files with S3 objects.

Detailed_Business_Rule: Walks the z/OS control-block chain (Prefixed Save Area to Task Control Block to Task I/O Table) using BPX-style address arithmetic to display the running JCL job name and step name and to enumerate every DD allocated to the job step. Each DD name is printed along with whether its underlying Unit Control Block is valid. This is a legacy demonstration feature with no equivalent in modern Java.
SQL_Decision_Control_Statements:
SET ADDRESS OF PSA-BLOCK   TO PSAPTR
SET ADDRESS OF TCB-BLOCK   TO TCB-POINT
SET ADDRESS OF TIOT-BLOCK  TO TIOT-POINT
SET TIOT-INDEX             TO TIOT-POINT
DISPLAY 'Running JCL : ' TIOTNJOB ' Step ' TIOTJSTP
PERFORM UNTIL END-OF-TIOT
    IF NOT NULL-UCB
        DISPLAY ': ' TIOCDDNM ' -- valid UCB'
    ELSE
        DISPLAY ': ' TIOCDDNM ' --  null UCB'
    END-IF
    SET ADDRESS OF TIOT-ENTRY TO TIOT-INDEX
END-PERFORM
Review_Comments: Mainframe-only feature: PSA/TCB/TIOT addressing. No equivalent in containerized Java. Drop entirely; if observability is needed in the modernized service expose Spring Boot Actuator /info and /env endpoints.

Detailed_Business_Rule: State-machine dispatcher. EVALUATE on the WS-FL-DD field selects the file to act on next. The legacy ALTER ... TO PROCEED TO statement dynamically rewrites the target of the GO TO 8100-FILE-OPEN paragraph so the same GO TO sends control to different OPEN paragraphs depending on the WS-FL-DD value. ALTER is dead-on-arrival in modern Java and must be refactored into an explicit switch / Map<String, Runnable>.
SQL_Decision_Control_Statements:
EVALUATE WS-FL-DD
  WHEN 'TRNXFILE'
    ALTER 8100-FILE-OPEN TO PROCEED TO 8100-TRNXFILE-OPEN
    GO TO 8100-FILE-OPEN
  WHEN 'XREFFILE'
    ALTER 8100-FILE-OPEN TO PROCEED TO 8200-XREFFILE-OPEN
    GO TO 8100-FILE-OPEN
  WHEN 'CUSTFILE'
    ALTER 8100-FILE-OPEN TO PROCEED TO 8300-CUSTFILE-OPEN
    GO TO 8100-FILE-OPEN
  WHEN 'ACCTFILE'
    ALTER 8100-FILE-OPEN TO PROCEED TO 8400-ACCTFILE-OPEN
    GO TO 8100-FILE-OPEN
  WHEN 'READTRNX'
    GO TO 8500-READTRNX-READ
  WHEN OTHER
    GO TO 9999-GOBACK
END-EVALUATE
Review_Comments: ALTER statement present (HIGH risk). GO TO statement present. Dynamic GO TO retargeting has no equivalent in modern Java. Refactor into explicit Map<String, Step> dispatch in Spring Batch flow definition.

Detailed_Business_Rule: Opens both report output files (text and HTML) for OUTPUT and initializes the buffered transaction-table workspace.
SQL_Decision_Control_Statements: OPEN OUTPUT STMT-FILE HTML-FILE\nINITIALIZE WS-TRNX-TABLE WS-TRN-TBL-CNTR

Detailed_Business_Rule: The single GO TO 8100-TRNXFILE-OPEN target whose destination is dynamically retargeted by the ALTER statements in 0000-START. This indirection means a single source-code line can branch to four different OPEN paragraphs depending on the runtime path.
SQL_Decision_Control_Statements: 8100-FILE-OPEN.\n    GO TO 8100-TRNXFILE-OPEN
Review_Comments: GO TO statement present. ALTER target.

Input_File_Layout: TRNX-CARD-NUM(PIC X(16))|TRNX-ID(PIC X(16))|TRNX-TYPE-CD(PIC X(02))|TRNX-CAT-CD(PIC 9(04))|TRNX-SOURCE(PIC X(10))|TRNX-DESC(PIC X(100))|TRNX-AMT(PIC S9(09)V99)|TRNX-MERCHANT-ID(PIC 9(09))|TRNX-MERCHANT-NAME(PIC X(50))|TRNX-MERCHANT-CITY(PIC X(50))|TRNX-MERCHANT-ZIP(PIC X(10))|TRNX-ORIG-TS(PIC X(26))|TRNX-PROC-TS(PIC X(26))|FILLER(PIC X(20))
Detailed_Business_Rule: Calls the CBSTM03B subroutine with operation 'O' (OPEN) on TRNXFILE then immediately with operation 'R' (READ) to fetch the first transaction record. Status codes 00 and 04 are both treated as success (file opened OK, possibly with informational warning); any other return code aborts the program. After the first read succeeds the program saves the card number, sets the dispatcher state to 'READTRNX' and GO TO's back to 0000-START to enter the buffered-table-fill loop.
SQL_Decision_Control_Statements:
MOVE 'TRNXFILE' TO WS-M03B-DD
SET M03B-OPEN TO TRUE
CALL 'CBSTM03B' USING WS-M03B-AREA
IF WS-M03B-RC = '00' OR '04'
    CONTINUE
ELSE
    PERFORM 9999-ABEND-PROGRAM
END-IF
SET M03B-READ TO TRUE
CALL 'CBSTM03B' USING WS-M03B-AREA
...
MOVE 'READTRNX' TO WS-FL-DD
GO TO 0000-START
Review_Comments: Multi-program CALL chain: CBSTM03A -> CBSTM03B. Inconsistent status accept '00' or '04'. GO TO statement to dispatcher.

Input_File_Layout: TRNX-CARD-NUM(PIC X(16))|TRNX-ID(PIC X(16))|TRNX-TYPE-CD(PIC X(02))|TRNX-CAT-CD(PIC 9(04))|TRNX-SOURCE(PIC X(10))|TRNX-DESC(PIC X(100))|TRNX-AMT(PIC S9(09)V99)|TRNX-MERCHANT-ID(PIC 9(09))|TRNX-MERCHANT-NAME(PIC X(50))|TRNX-MERCHANT-CITY(PIC X(50))|TRNX-MERCHANT-ZIP(PIC X(10))|TRNX-ORIG-TS(PIC X(26))|TRNX-PROC-TS(PIC X(26))|FILLER(PIC X(20))
Detailed_Business_Rule: Buffered-transaction-table fill loop. While reading TRNXFILE sequentially the program tracks the previous card number; while the same card is being read the per-card transaction counter (TR-CNT) is incremented; when the card changes the program stores the prior card's transaction count, advances the card counter (CR-CNT), and resets the transaction counter. Each transaction is stashed into the in-memory 51-by-10 two-dimensional table WS-TRNX-TABLE indexed by card and by transaction-within-card. On EOF (status 10) the program stores the final card's transaction count, switches the dispatcher to 'XREFFILE', and GO TO's back to 0000-START. On any other status the program aborts.
SQL_Decision_Control_Statements:
8500-READTRNX-READ.
    IF WS-SAVE-CARD = TRNX-CARD-NUM
        ADD 1 TO TR-CNT
    ELSE
        MOVE TR-CNT TO WS-TRCT (CR-CNT)
        ADD 1 TO CR-CNT
        MOVE 1 TO TR-CNT
    END-IF
    MOVE TRNX-CARD-NUM TO WS-CARD-NUM (CR-CNT)
    MOVE TRNX-ID TO WS-TRAN-NUM (CR-CNT, TR-CNT)
    MOVE TRNX-REST TO WS-TRAN-REST (CR-CNT, TR-CNT)
    MOVE TRNX-CARD-NUM TO WS-SAVE-CARD
    SET M03B-READ TO TRUE
    CALL 'CBSTM03B' USING WS-M03B-AREA
    EVALUATE WS-M03B-RC
      WHEN '00'
        MOVE WS-M03B-FLDT TO TRNX-RECORD
        GO TO 8500-READTRNX-READ
      WHEN '10'
        GO TO 8599-EXIT
      WHEN OTHER
        PERFORM 9999-ABEND-PROGRAM
    END-EVALUATE
Review_Comments: Hardcoded threshold: WS-CARD-TBL OCCURS 51 TIMES, WS-TRAN-TBL OCCURS 10 TIMES. Cards beyond 51 or transactions beyond 10 per card are silently discarded. Replace with java.util.ArrayList in Java migration. Recursive GO TO 8500-READTRNX-READ implements the read loop.

Detailed_Business_Rule: Calls CBSTM03B to OPEN XREFFILE then transitions the dispatcher to 'CUSTFILE' so the next 0000-START iteration opens the customer file.
SQL_Decision_Control_Statements:
MOVE 'XREFFILE' TO WS-M03B-DD
SET M03B-OPEN TO TRUE

Detailed_Business_Rule: Statement-creation main loop. Reads the next cross-reference record, then looks up the customer record by customer-id and the account record by account-id, then calls 5000-CREATE-STATEMENT to write 16 statement text lines for the new account holder, resets the per-card running total to zero, and calls 4000-TRNXFILE-GET to walk the in-memory buffered transaction table for this card and emit one detail row per matched transaction. Continues until cross-reference EOF.
SQL_Decision_Control_Statements:
PERFORM UNTIL END-OF-FILE = 'Y'
    IF END-OF-FILE = 'N'
        PERFORM 1000-XREFFILE-GET-NEXT
        IF END-OF-FILE = 'N'
            PERFORM 2000-CUSTFILE-GET
            PERFORM 3000-ACCTFILE-GET
            PERFORM 5000-CREATE-STATEMENT
            MOVE 1 TO CR-JMP
            MOVE ZERO TO WS-TOTAL-AMT
            PERFORM 4000-TRNXFILE-GET
        END-IF
    END-IF
END-PERFORM

Detailed_Business_Rule: Reads the next sequential cross-reference record via the CBSTM03B subroutine. EVALUATE on the return code: 00 success, 10 sets EOF flag and ends the main loop, any other code displays an error and aborts the program.
SQL_Decision_Control_Statements:
MOVE 'XREFFILE' TO WS-M03B-DD
SET M03B-READ TO TRUE
CALL 'CBSTM03B' USING WS-M03B-AREA
EVALUATE WS-M03B-RC
  WHEN '00'
    CONTINUE
  WHEN '10'
    MOVE 'Y' TO END-OF-FILE
  WHEN OTHER
    PERFORM 9999-ABEND-PROGRAM
END-EVALUATE
MOVE WS-M03B-FLDT TO CARD-XREF-RECORD

Detailed_Business_Rule: Reads the customer master by 9-digit customer ID via the CBSTM03B subroutine using operation K (READ-K, keyed read). The key length is computed as the COBOL LENGTH OF intrinsic of XREF-CUST-ID. Any non-zero return code aborts the program.

  WHEN '00' CONTINUE
  WHEN OTHER PERFORM 9999-ABEND-PROGRAM

Detailed_Business_Rule: Walks the in-memory transaction table starting at CR-JMP looking for the row whose card number matches the cross-reference's card number. Loop terminates early when the table card number exceeds the cross-reference's card number (the table is sorted by card so any subsequent rows belong to a later card).
SQL_Decision_Control_Statements: PERFORM VARYING CR-JMP FROM 1 BY 1\n  UNTIL CR-JMP > CR-CNT\n  OR (WS-CARD-NUM (CR-JMP) > XREF-CARD-NUM)

Linkage_Columns: WS-TRAN-NUM(CR-JMP, TR-JMP) -> TRNX-ID; WS-TRAN-REST(CR-JMP, TR-JMP) -> TRNX-REST
Detailed_Business_Rule: For the matching card, iterates from 1 to the per-card transaction count (WS-TRCT(CR-JMP)) and writes one detail line for each transaction. Each detail writes the TRNX-ID, TRNX-DESC, TRNX-AMT into the formatted statement detail row (ST-LINE14 in the text statement, plus three HTML cells in the HTML statement) and accumulates the amount into the per-card running total.
SQL_Decision_Control_Statements:
IF XREF-CARD-NUM = WS-CARD-NUM (CR-JMP)
    MOVE WS-CARD-NUM (CR-JMP) TO TRNX-CARD-NUM
    PERFORM VARYING TR-JMP FROM 1 BY 1
      UNTIL (TR-JMP > WS-TRCT (CR-JMP))
        MOVE WS-TRAN-NUM (CR-JMP, TR-JMP) TO TRNX-ID
        MOVE WS-TRAN-REST (CR-JMP, TR-JMP) TO TRNX-REST
        PERFORM 6000-WRITE-TRANS
        ADD TRNX-AMT TO WS-TOTAL-AMT
    END-PERFORM
END-IF
Review_Comments: Packed-decimal COMP-3 arithmetic on WS-TOTAL-AMT (PIC S9(09)V99); map to BigDecimal in Java migration.

Detailed_Business_Rule: File-service subroutine entry point. EVALUATE on the LK-M03B-DD field selects which file processing paragraph to invoke: TRNXFILE, XREFFILE, CUSTFILE, or ACCTFILE. Any other value falls through to 9999-GOBACK without doing anything.
SQL_Decision_Control_Statements:
EVALUATE LK-M03B-DD
  WHEN 'TRNXFILE'
    PERFORM 1000-TRNXFILE-PROC THRU 1999-EXIT
  WHEN 'XREFFILE'
    PERFORM 2000-XREFFILE-PROC THRU 2999-EXIT
  WHEN 'CUSTFILE'
    PERFORM 3000-CUSTFILE-PROC THRU 3999-EXIT
  WHEN 'ACCTFILE'
    PERFORM 4000-ACCTFILE-PROC THRU 4999-EXIT
  WHEN OTHER
    GO TO 9999-GOBACK

    GO TO 3900-EXIT
END-IF

Detailed_Business_Rule: For every daily transaction read, the program echoes the input record to SYSOUT, performs an XREF lookup using the card number, and if the card was found performs an account lookup using the cross-reference's account ID. If the account is not found a diagnostic 'ACCOUNT <id> NOT FOUND' is displayed. If the card is not found a diagnostic 'CARD NUMBER <num> COULD NOT BE VERIFIED. SKIPPING TRANSACTION ID-<id>' is displayed and the transaction is skipped silently.
SQL_Decision_Control_Statements:
PERFORM UNTIL END-OF-DAILY-TRANS-FILE = 'Y'
    PERFORM 1000-DALYTRAN-GET-NEXT
    DISPLAY DALYTRAN-RECORD
    MOVE 0 TO WS-XREF-READ-STATUS
    MOVE DALYTRAN-CARD-NUM TO XREF-CARD-NUM
    PERFORM 2000-LOOKUP-XREF
    IF WS-XREF-READ-STATUS = 0
      PERFORM 3000-READ-ACCOUNT
      IF WS-ACCT-READ-STATUS NOT = 0
          DISPLAY 'ACCOUNT ' ACCT-ID ' NOT FOUND'
      END-IF
    ELSE
      DISPLAY 'CARD NUMBER ' DALYTRAN-CARD-NUM
        ' COULD NOT BE VERIFIED. SKIPPING TRANSACTION ID-'
        DALYTRAN-ID
    END-IF
END-PERFORM

Rule_Number: # Modernization Mapping (plain text trailer; not part of CSV row data)
This trailer is informational only and is intentionally placed below the last
data row separated by a blank line so csv.reader / Excel / LibreOffice Calc
treat it as a comment block. The canonical Markdown-formatted version of the
Modernization Mapping appears in CardDemo_Batch_BRE.md section 3.
Each batch COBOL program maps to a Glue PySpark job using DynamicFrame for sequential
file I/O and JDBC for reads from PostgreSQL. CBTRN02C (POSTTRAN) becomes a Glue job
with two DynamicFrame reads and one upsert write. CBACT04C (INTCALC) becomes a Glue
job whose hardcoded PARM date becomes a job parameter --parm-date. COMBTRAN's SORT +
REPRO chain becomes DataFrame.union().orderBy('tran_id').write + an INSERT ON CONFLICT
upsert. CBTRN03C (TRANREPT) becomes a Glue job with broadcast joins on the small
lookup tables (TRANTYPE/TRANCATG) and an Athena CTAS for the page totals. CREASTMT
becomes a Glue PySpark job with a per-card groupBy().apply() that builds the HTML +
TXT statements from a Jinja2 template. PRTCATBL becomes a Glue job that JDBC-reads
The four read utilities (READACCT/CARD/CUST/XREF) are replaced by direct SELECT queries
or by Glue Notebooks; they are not production batch jobs in the modernized service.
Section 3.2 - Java ECS Batch (Spring Batch) Mapping
Each program maps to a Spring Batch Job. CBTRN02C becomes a chunk-oriented Step with
handled by SkipPolicy. CBACT04C becomes a chunk-oriented Step with JdbcCursorItemReader
interest transactions and post interest to the account. COMBTRAN becomes a Tasklet
running INSERT ... ON CONFLICT against PostgreSQL. CBTRN03C becomes a chunk-oriented
Step with a FlatFileFooterCallback for page totals. CREASTMT becomes a three-Step Job
PRTCATBL becomes a two-Step Job. The four read utilities become read-only REST endpoints.
CBTRN01C is an orphan program that should be deleted or restored as a dry-run preview
Risk 1 (HIGH): ALTER + GO TO state machine in CBSTM03A. Self-modifying control flow
has no Java equivalent. Mitigation: replace with explicit Spring Batch Flow of named
Risk 2 (HIGH): Hardcoded date and DSN literals across the pipeline. PARM '2022071800'

Job_Name: VOLUMES(TSU023) in CREASTMT IDCAMS DEFINE. Mitigation:

Rule_Number: externalize into Spring Boot application.yml profiles and Spring Batch JobParameter.
Risk 3 (MEDIUM): Inconsistent file-status accept lists '00' or '23'. Treats record-
not-found as success in some places. Mitigation: explicit Optional<T> return types
and per-call-site decision on whether to default-create or throw.
Risk 4 (MEDIUM): Packed-decimal COMP-3 arithmetic across all monetary fields. Naive
Java translation with double/float introduces rounding errors. Mitigation: BigDecimal
property-based tests comparing legacy vs modernized output byte-for-byte.
Risk 5 (LOW): Hardcoded magic values for system-generated records. CBACT04C TRAN-TYPE
by 10-tran array bound. Mitigation: externalize into a system_transaction_kind
reference table and replace static arrays with java.util.ArrayList.
